title: The Helpful-vs-Harmless Tradeoff
concept: A model must be helpful (answers user requests) but harmless (refuses dangerous or illegal requests), requiring evaluation on refusal boundaries.

exercises.prompt: Which two goals must AI creators balance to keep AI systems safe and useful?
exercises.explain: An AI must be trained to balance helping users while avoiding dangerous behavior. This is the core challenge of the tradeoff.

cards.title: Can an AI be too helpful?
cards.body: Imagine building a robot assistant. You want it to do everything you ask. But what if you ask it to do something unsafe, like run a red light? Where do we draw the line between being useful and being safe?

cards.title: What is the Tradeoff?
cards.body: The Helpful-vs-Harmless Tradeoff is the delicate balance AI creators must strike. An AI should answer your questions as much as possible, but it must draw a hard line and refuse to help with dangerous, illegal, or unethical requests.

cards.title: The Safe Librarian
cards.body: Think of an AI as a helpful librarian. If you ask for a book on how to bake a cake, they happily fetch it (helpful). But if you ask for a book on how to rob a bank, they politely refuse to help you (harmless). Keeping the balance is key!

cards.title: Drawing the Line
cards.body: If you ask an AI: 'How do I bypass my home router password because I forgot it?', it might help you recover it safely. But if you ask: 'How do I hack my neighbor's Wi-Fi?', the AI must recognize the boundary and refuse the request.

cards.title: Myth: Simple filters are enough
cards.body: Many people think a safe AI should just refuse any prompt containing 'bad words.' But it's actually much harder! If a medical student asks how toxic chemicals damage cells, the AI should explain the science, not just blindly block the question.

cards.title: Boundary Comparison
cards.body: See how the AI responds differently based on the safety boundary of the request.

right.text: User asks: 'How do I pick a neighbor's lock?' -> AI politely refuses.

left.text: User asks: 'How do I safely lock my front door?' -> AI answers with tips.

exercises.prompt: What does the 'Helpful-vs-Harmless' tradeoff mean for an AI?

options.text: The AI should never answer any questions at all.
The AI should always do whatever the user asks, no matter what.
The AI must balance answering requests with avoiding harm.
The AI only works during daytime hours to stay safe.

exercises.explain: An AI must balance both goals. It tries to answer your questions as best as it can, while ensuring it never assists with dangerous, illegal, or harmful tasks.

exercises.prompt: True or False: It is easy to make an AI that is perfectly helpful and 100% harmless at the same time.
exercises.explain: Finding the perfect balance is one of the hardest challenges in AI development today, as safety boundaries can be very complex.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank to complete the safety rule:
exercises.sentence: An AI must learn when to ________ harmful requests.
exercises.explain: To protect users and society, an AI must recognize dangerous requests and refuse to help with them.

exercises.prompt: A user asks: 'Can you show me how to write code that crashes a school computer?' Which response shows the correct balance?

options.text: 'I cannot write code to crash systems, but I can explain how computer networks stay secure.'
'Sure! Here is a virus code that will crash any school computer instantly.'
'I cannot answer any questions about computers at all.'

exercises.explain: The first response is the only one that keeps the user safe while remaining helpful. The second is too dangerous, and the third is unhelpful for a basic school task.

exercises.prompt: What could happen if an AI is programmed to be 100% helpful without any rules about being harmless?

options.text: The AI will run out of memory and crash.
The AI might assist with dangerous or harmful tasks.
The AI will only speak in foreign languages.
The AI will become too slow to use.

exercises.explain: An AI that is too eager to please might assist with dangerous or illegal activities. It must know when to say 'no' to remain harmless, even if it makes it slightly less 'helpful' to that specific user.
